title: Indellian

'''Indellian''' was a low-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] that served as a [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] world, [[Factory world/Legends|manufacturing hub]], and government center for the [[Yarith sector/Legends|Yarith sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was originally settled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as commerce developed around the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] in the Outer Rim's [[Greater Javin/Legends|Greater Javin]] region. Indellian's placement along the zigzagging border between the Yarith and [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sectors]] was part of an intentional Republic effort to cut off neighboring [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] and check local [[Politics/Legends|political]] power.

==Description==
A low-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] industrial [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]],<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> Indellian was the primary world of the [[Indellian system/Legends|Indellian system]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|99|Indellian}}</ref> which was tucked inside the meandering border of the [[Yarith sector/Legends|Yarith sector]]. This area in turn belonged to the [[Greater Javin]], a region<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion<ref name="Atlas" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Indellian represented the Yarith [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] first stopover for Rimward travel along the major [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] and also stood as the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Coreward]] entry point for the backwater [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

==History==
A [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Corellian Trade Spine spanned only as far as the edge of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], while Indellian and other worlds lying beyond were considered then to be part of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. Only [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and the most daring [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] visited this imperfectly surveyed [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] frontier in hopes of tapping into its economic potential. However, the vast [[Twin Nebulae/Legends|Twin Nebulae]] [[Nebula/Legends|gas clouds]] greatly hampered regional [[Astrogation/Legends|navigation]] and prevented countless [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorers from expanding the Trade Spine's commercial reach.<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] transformed the local [[Politics/Legends|political]] landscape after defeating the region's native [[Mugaari]] in a brief, one-sided [[Galactic Republic–Mugaari war|war]], resulting in the establishment of the sprawling [[Javin sector/Legends|Javin sector]] in the Outer Rim and the colonization of several worlds running down the Trade Spine, including Indellian. Staging from these newly settled worlds, Republic traders continued the search for a hyperspace shortcut through the Twin Nebulae, but [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] passed before the entrepreneur [[Ecclessis Figg/Legends|Ecclessis Figg]] finally succeeded.<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

Thanks to Figg's new hyperspace routes, the Twin Nebulae region exploded into an economic powerhouse. The quiet [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gerrenthum/Legends|Gerrenthum]] thus found itself at the nexus of three profitable trade routes and grew into a bustling [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] world, while Indellian and its neighbors followed suit. Indellian was reorganized into the Republic's new [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]]<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> circa [[400 BBY/Legends|400 BBY]],<ref name="Date">"[[Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin]]," which is set during the [[Rebellion era|Rebellion]] and [[New Republic era]]s ([[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]–[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]; see {{Blog|holocron|24|Star Wars Eras}}), states that the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]] was established in the last [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of [[Ecclessis Figg/Legends|Ecclessis Figg's]] life. The text states that Figg [[Death/Legends|died]] nearly four [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to its setting, thus providing an approximate date of [[400 BBY/Legends|400 BBY]] for the Anoat [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] establishment.</ref> but generations later it became part of the new Yarith sector<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />—the Republic deliberately drew the zigzagging border between Yarith and Anoat to cut off neighboring worlds politically and blunt local power. Even so, Indellian developed into a trade world, [[Factory world/Legends|manufacturing hub]], and government center for the Yarith sector.<ref name="Atlas" />

==Behind the scenes==
Indellian was created for the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] article "[[Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin]]," an [[Wizards.com|online]] supplement for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> This drew upon the preexisting Indellian [[Star system/Legends|system]], which originated in the [[1989]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' adventure ''[[Crisis on Cloud City (adventure book)|Crisis on Cloud City]]''.<ref name="Crisis on Cloud City">[[Crisis on Cloud City (adventure book)|''Crisis on Cloud City'']]</ref> The [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''<ref name="CSWE" /> and [[2009]] ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' further developed Indellian's [[Canon|lore]].<ref name="Atlas" />

''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' mistakenly refers to Indellian as a planet and states that it "was located in the Yarith sector, where it intersected with the Javin and Anoat sectors to create the area known as the Greater Javin."<ref name="CSWE" /> However, "Galactic Gazetter" and ''The Essential Atlas'' each identify Indellian as a planetoid and feature Greater Javin maps that depict the intersection between the Javin, Anoat, and Yarith sectors to be Coreward of the planet [[Mijos/Legends|Mijos]] and off the Corellian Trade Spine, some distance from Indellian.<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /><ref name="Atlas" />
